evals.prompt: I need to add a Supplier model to sabsys. Suppliers belong to an organization, have a name, RNC (tax ID, optional), phone, email, and a status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE, default ACTIVE). Give me the full stack: model, form, filter, views (list with datatable, detail, create inline on list, edit via HTMX modal, delete with guard), URLs, the three template files (list, row partial, modal form), admin registration, a factory, and basic view tests.
evals.expected_output: Complete, copy-pasteable Django files for a Supplier feature: models.py with ERPBaseModel + org FK + TextChoices status, forms.py with crispy FormHelper form_tag=False, filters.py with fts_search, views.py with ERPBaseViewMixin + DataTableMixin list + refresh_table classmethod + HTMX-aware update/delete, urls.py with uuid pk, three HTML templates using Bootstrap 5 and HTMX, admin.py with @admin.register decorator, tests/factories.py with @mute_signals, and tests/test_views.py with _login helper and org-scoped assertions.

assertions.name: model_inherits_ERPBaseModel
assertions.description: The generated model.py imports and inherits from ERPBaseModel

assertions.name: model_has_organization_fk
assertions.description: The model has an 'organization' ForeignKey to 'accounts.Organization'

assertions.name: model_has_status_textchoices
assertions.description: Status field uses TextChoices inner class, not plain CharField with choices tuple

assertions.name: views_use_ERPBaseViewMixin
assertions.description: All views inherit ERPBaseViewMixin (not just LoginRequiredMixin directly)

assertions.name: list_view_uses_DataTableMixin
assertions.description: The list view mixes in DataTableMixin and defines dt_columns, dt_url, dt_row_template

assertions.name: list_view_scopes_to_org
assertions.description: QuerySet uses .for_org(request.organization) not .filter(organization=...)

assertions.name: delete_view_is_post_only
assertions.description: Delete view only defines a post() method, not get()

assertions.name: delete_view_admin_required
assertions.description: Delete view has admin_required = True

assertions.name: htmx_refresh_table_classmethod
assertions.description: List view has a refresh_table classmethod that returns HX-Trigger showToast

assertions.name: plain_view_uses_self_get_context
assertions.description: Detail/update views that are plain View subclasses call self.get_context() not get_context_data()

assertions.name: urls_use_uuid_pk
assertions.description: urls.py uses <uuid:pk> path converter, not <int:pk>

assertions.name: factory_mutes_signals
assertions.description: Factory class is decorated with @mute_signals(post_save)

assertions.name: test_login_sets_session
assertions.description: Test _login helper calls force_login AND sets session['active_org_slug']

assertions.name: templates_in_spanish
assertions.description: Template labels and button text are in Spanish (e.g. 'Guardar', 'Cancelar', 'Eliminar')

assertions.name: form_tag_false
assertions.description: Form's __init__ sets self.helper.form_tag = False for modal compatibility

evals.prompt: Add a PurchaseOrder app to sabsys. A purchase order has: supplier (FK to a Supplier model in the same app), organization FK, number (auto-generated, read-only), status (DRAFT, CONFIRMED, RECEIVED, CANCELLED), issue_date, expected_date (optional), and notes. I need a PurchaseOrderService with confirm() and receive() methods that handle the status transitions atomically. Also give me the full CRUD views, URLs, templates, and tests for the service transitions.
evals.expected_output: A service class with @transaction.atomic methods that raise ValueError on invalid transitions. Views that call the service and never mutate status directly. Tests that verify the status machine using factories.

assertions.name: service_uses_transaction_atomic
assertions.description: Service methods are decorated with @transaction.atomic

assertions.name: service_raises_value_error
assertions.description: Service raises ValueError (not Http errors) when transition is invalid

assertions.name: views_call_service
assertions.description: Views call the service (e.g. PurchaseOrderService.confirm(order)) rather than mutating status directly

assertions.name: model_has_org_fk
assertions.description: PurchaseOrder has organization ForeignKey to 'accounts.Organization'

assertions.name: service_tests_use_factories
assertions.description: Service tests create objects via factories, not Model.objects.create

assertions.name: transition_test_checks_status
assertions.description: Tests refresh_from_db() after calling the service and assert the new status

evals.prompt: I want to add expense tracking to sabsys. Create an Expense model with: organization, category (FK to ExpenseCategory, also needs to be created), amount (DecimalField), date, description, status (PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED), and approved_by (FK to User, nullable). ExpenseCategory just needs org + name. Both need full CRUD. The expense list should show category name, amount with intcomma formatting, and status badge colored by status. Delete should be blocked if the expense is APPROVED.
evals.expected_output: Two models (ExpenseCategory + Expense) both inheriting ERPBaseModel with org FK. Expense delete view checks status before deleting and returns showSwal if blocked. Row template uses intcomma filter and Bootstrap badge classes colored by status. ExpenseCategory has its own minimal CRUD.

assertions.name: two_models_with_org_fk
assertions.description: Both ExpenseCategory and Expense inherit ERPBaseModel and have organization FK

assertions.name: expense_has_category_fk
assertions.description: Expense model has a ForeignKey to ExpenseCategory

assertions.name: delete_blocked_for_approved
assertions.description: ExpenseDeleteView checks expense.status == APPROVED and returns error before deleting

assertions.name: blocked_delete_returns_showswal
assertions.description: Blocked delete returns HX-Trigger with showSwal when request.htmx is True

assertions.name: row_uses_intcomma
assertions.description: Row partial template uses the intcomma filter for amount display

assertions.name: status_badge_by_status
assertions.description: Row template renders different Bootstrap badge classes depending on status value
